# Boring Meeting Alarm

> A free browser tool that listens to the audio of a meeting tab you share via screen sharing, and alerts you with a modal, system notification, alarm sound, and tab title flash when the audio stays silent longer than a duration you configure. Designed for remote video calls where the host wraps up while you have stepped away.

Everything runs locally in the browser. No backend, no recording, no upload — audio is analyzed entirely in-page via the Web Audio API.

## How it works

The page captures the audio stream of a user-selected tab via `navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia({ audio: true })`. The stream is routed through an `AnalyserNode` (`fftSize=2048`) and polled every 200ms to compute the RMS level in dB. When the level stays below a user-configurable threshold (-80 to -20 dB, default -50) for a user-configurable duration (5 to 3600 seconds, default 30), the page fires:

- An in-page modal alert
- A synthesized alarm tone (OscillatorNode + GainNode, opt-in)
- A `Notification` system notification (opt-in, requires permission)
- A flashing tab title

A volume bar with live dB readout helps users pick a threshold while talking.

## Browser support

- **Chrome / Edge / Brave / Arc / other Chromium-based browsers**: fully supported
- **Firefox**: not supported — Firefox does not provide a "Share tab audio" option in its `getDisplayMedia` dialog
- **Safari**: limited and not officially tested
- HTTPS required (production is fine; `localhost` is a secure context for development)

### Posts

- How we built a meeting silence detector in 80 lines of Web Audio — https://alarm.tokugai.com/blog/meeting-silence-detector-web-audio/

## Pricing

Free. No accounts. No tracking beyond opt-in Google Analytics. Hosted on Cloudflare Workers Static Assets.

## Privacy

- All audio analysis happens locally in the browser via Web Audio API
- No audio stream is uploaded, recorded, or transmitted
- Google Analytics 4 (cookieless by default via Consent Mode v2; full tracking only after explicit user consent)
- Google AdSense (non-personalized by default; personalized only after explicit consent)

## Source code

https://github.com/lexluthor0304/boring_meeting_alarm — MIT licensed.

Built by tokugai. Last updated 2026-05-13.
